title: trifolium eriocephalum subsp. cusickii
intro: Trifolium eriocephalum subsp. cusickii, also known as Cusick's clover or western buffalo clover, is a perennial legume native to the western United States. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its flavonoid and phenolic compounds, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Trifolium eriocephalum subsp. cusickii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive well-being. Traditionally, it is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are often consumed to promote overall health and well-being.
